chunks.text: Prasad Raghavendra is a Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office location at 623 Soda Hall and email prasad@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are held on Wednesdays from 11 AM to noon at 623 Soda. He has a research assistant named Eva Leach with email eva.leach@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Prasad Raghavendra's position and contact information?
chunks.answer: Professor at UC Berkeley EECS department.

chunks.text: Prasad Raghavendra earned his PhD in Computer Science and Engineering from University of Washington, Seattle in 2009. He completed his M.S. in the same field at University of Washington in 2007. His undergraduate degree is a B.S. in Computer Science from Indian Institute of Technology, Madras, India in 2005.
chunks.question: What are Prasad Raghavendra's educational qualifications?
chunks.answer: PhD from University of Washington in 2009.

chunks.text: Prasad Raghavendra's research areas include Theory (THY). He is affiliated with research centers such as Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) and Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: What are Prasad Raghavendra's research areas and centers?
chunks.answer: Theory research at CLIMB and SITC centers.

chunks.text: Selected publications include works on sparse cuts via higher eigenvalues (STOC 2012), bypassing UGC for geometric inapproximability (SODA 2012), approximating CSPs with global constraints (SODA 2012), rounding SDP hierarchies via global correlation (FOCS 2011), and beating random ordering hardness (SIAM Journal 2011).
chunks.question: What are some of Prasad Raghavendra's key publications?
chunks.answer: Research on CSPs, SDP hierarchies, and inapproximability.

chunks.text: Additional publications include list decoding tensor products (SIAM Journal 2011), graph expansion and unique games conjecture (STOC 2010), coarse differentiation in planar graphs (2010), computing Grothendieck constant (SODA 2009), rounding any CSP (FOCS 2009), and hardness of learning halfspaces with noise (SIAM Journal 2009).
chunks.question: What other research topics has Prasad Raghavendra published on?
chunks.answer: Graph theory, learning theory, and CSP rounding.

chunks.text: Further publications cover integrality gaps for SDP relaxations (FOCS 2009), agnostic learning of monomials (FOCS 2009), hardness of sparse linear systems (2009), optimal algorithms for CSPs (STOC 2008), SDP gaps for multiway cut problems (STOC 2008), and locally decodable codes (2007).
chunks.question: What are some additional publications by Prasad Raghavendra?
chunks.answer: Works on SDP gaps, learning algorithms, and codes.

chunks.text: Prasad Raghavendra has received several honors and awards including the Michael and Sheila Held Prize in 2018, Okawa Research Grant in 2015, NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) in 2013, and Sloan Research Fellowship in 2012.
chunks.question: What awards has Prasad Raghavendra received?
chunks.answer: Held Prize, Okawa Grant, CAREER Award, Sloan Fellowship.
